name: Las Cruces
headline: Landscaping Services in Las Cruces, NM
intro: Las Cruces is our home. We've spent nearly two decades building landscapes that don't just survive Doña Ana County's desert climate — they thrive in it. From adobe courtyards in Mesilla to the modern homes of Sonoma Ranch, every property we touch is planned around the soil, sun, and monsoon patterns of this region.
localContext: Our crew is based right here in Las Cruces. We show up fast, know which plants hold up near the Organ Mountains, and understand the local permit process. No out-of-town contractors. No generic plant lists.

neighborhoods.name: Sonoma Ranch
neighborhoods.description: Modern homes with HOA guidelines. We handle clean-lined hardscaping, native plantings, and work within the architectural review process.

neighborhoods.name: Picacho Hills
neighborhoods.description: Estate lots on west-facing slopes. We build retaining walls, manage erosion, and design yards that preserve your mountain views.

neighborhoods.name: Mesilla Historic District
neighborhoods.description: Adobe courtyards and walled gardens near the Plaza. Stone pathways, drought-tolerant natives, and materials that respect the village character.

neighborhoods.name: University Hills / NMSU
neighborhoods.description: Mid-century ranch homes near campus. We replace water-hungry lawns with xeriscape and upgrade aging irrigation systems.

neighborhoods.name: Telshor / East Mesa
neighborhoods.description: Homes east of I-25 that catch heavy wind. We plant windbreaks, install gravel mulch, and build drip systems that handle the gusts.

neighborhoods.name: Organ Mountain Foothills
neighborhoods.description: Large desert lots near the Organs. Fire-wise plantings, monsoon drainage grading, and designs that work with the natural terrain.

name: Mesilla
headline: Landscaping Services in Mesilla, NM
intro: Mesilla's adobe architecture and historic character call for landscaping that fits — not a cookie-cutter suburban yard. We work with the walled courtyards, narrow lots, and traditional materials that make this community unique.
localContext: Just minutes from our Las Cruces base, we've handled projects all around the Mesilla Plaza and surrounding residential streets. We understand the aesthetic expectations here and choose plants and hardscape materials that complement the village feel.

name: Mesilla Park
headline: Landscaping Services in Mesilla Park, NM
intro: Mesilla Park sits between Las Cruces and Mesilla with a mix of established homes and newer builds. The lots tend to be generous, and many homeowners here are looking to reduce water use without giving up a good-looking yard.
localContext: We're right down the road. Whether you need a full xeriscape conversion, a new patio, or sprinkler repairs, we can be on site the same week you call.

name: Doña Ana
headline: Landscaping Services in Doña Ana, NM
intro: Doña Ana is a small community north of Las Cruces with rural properties and agricultural land transitioning to residential. The soil here is heavy on caliche, and irrigation planning is critical for anything you plant.
localContext: We've worked properties throughout Doña Ana and understand the unique challenges — larger lots, well water systems, and wind exposure from the open valley floor.

name: Organ
tagline: Landscaping at the Base of the Organs
headline: Landscaping Services in Organ, NM
intro: Organ sits at the base of the Organ Mountains with rocky terrain, sloped lots, and some of the best views in the valley. Landscaping here means working with the natural grade, managing monsoon runoff, and choosing plants that handle the elevation.
localContext: We've handled projects in Organ that required retaining walls on steep grades, erosion control for monsoon season, and fire-wise plant selections for properties bordering BLM land.

name: Anthony
headline: Landscaping Services in Anthony, NM
intro: Anthony straddles the New Mexico-Texas border south of Las Cruces. Properties here range from new subdivisions to older homes on larger agricultural lots, and the summer heat is as intense as anywhere in the valley.
localContext: We make the drive to Anthony regularly. The soil and climate are similar to Las Cruces but with even more heat exposure, which makes xeriscaping and efficient irrigation especially important here.

name: Sunland Park
headline: Landscaping Services in Sunland Park, NM
intro: Sunland Park is the southernmost community in New Mexico, just west of El Paso. The area has seen steady residential growth, and homeowners here deal with the same desert conditions as Las Cruces — plus extra wind off the mesa.
localContext: We serve Sunland Park with the same services we offer in Las Cruces. Xeriscaping, hardscaping, irrigation, and artificial turf are all popular here given the heat and wind.

name: Picacho Hills
headline: Landscaping Services in Picacho Hills, NM
intro: Picacho Hills is a master-planned community west of Las Cruces with larger estate lots, mountain views, and an HOA that maintains design standards. The west-facing slopes get intense afternoon sun and need landscapes that can take the heat.
localContext: We work in Picacho Hills regularly and know the HOA guidelines. Terraced retaining walls, native plantings, and irrigation systems sized for the slopes are what most homeowners here need.

name: Sonoma Ranch
headline: Landscaping Services in Sonoma Ranch, NM
intro: Sonoma Ranch is one of Las Cruces' newer master-planned communities with modern homes, an active HOA, and strict architectural guidelines. Landscapes here tend toward clean lines, low-water plantings, and hardscape-forward designs.
localContext: We've completed multiple projects in Sonoma Ranch and are familiar with the architectural review process. We design within the HOA rules and handle the paperwork so you don't have to.

name: East Mesa
headline: Landscaping Services in East Mesa, NM
intro: East Mesa covers the growing residential area east of I-25 toward the Organ Mountains. Properties here face serious wind exposure, and the soil is sandy with heavy caliche underneath. Landscapes need to be tough.
localContext: We build windbreaks, install gravel mulch systems, and design irrigation that handles the gusts. Artificial turf is especially popular on East Mesa for homeowners tired of fighting the wind and dust.

name: Las Alturas
headline: Landscaping Services in Las Alturas, NM
intro: Las Alturas is a quiet residential area in the southern foothills of Las Cruces with established homes and mature landscapes. Many homeowners here are updating aging yards — replacing thirsty lawns with xeriscape and modernizing old irrigation systems.
localContext: We handle full yard renovations and targeted upgrades in Las Alturas. The area's mature trees and existing hardscape often just need smart updates rather than complete teardowns.

name: Talavera
headline: Landscaping Services in Talavera, NM
intro: Talavera is a growing residential area northeast of Las Cruces with newer homes on larger lots. The open terrain means wind and sun exposure are significant factors in any landscape plan.
localContext: We design landscapes for Talavera properties that account for the wind, use native plants that handle full sun, and include irrigation systems that don't waste water on a windy day.

name: University Hills
headline: Landscaping Services in University Hills, NM
intro: University Hills is an established neighborhood near NMSU with 1960s through 1980s ranch-style homes. Many yards here have old shade trees, outdated irrigation, and lawns that cost a fortune to water.
localContext: We specialize in converting these older yards — removing what's not working, replacing lawns with xeriscape, upgrading to drip irrigation, and giving these homes a fresh look that cuts the water bill.

name: Dripping Springs
headline: Landscaping Services in Dripping Springs, NM
intro: Dripping Springs sits in the foothills east of Las Cruces near the Organ Mountains-Desert Peaks National Monument. Properties here tend to be on larger lots with natural desert terrain, rocky soil, and elevation changes.
localContext: We work with the natural landscape rather than against it — fire-wise plant choices, monsoon drainage grading, and hardscaping that blends with the desert surroundings. These lots need a different approach than a typical suburban yard.
